title: Generic math: static abstract члены и INumber<T>

cards.prompt: A single generic Add<T> where T : INumber<T> works for every numeric type: int gives 7, double gives 4 (2.5+1.5=4.0 prints 4), long gives 30, so the output is 7 4 30
cards.code: using System; using System.Numerics; static T Add<T>(T a, T b) where T : INumber<T> => a + b; Console.WriteLine($"{Add(3, 4)} {Add(2.5, 1.5)} {Add(10L, 20L)}");

cards.prompt: T.Zero is a static abstract member of INumberBase<T>; a generic Sum over an int[] gives 10 and over a double[] gives 7 (1.5+2.5+3.0=7.0 prints 7), so the output is 10 7
cards.code: using System; using System.Numerics; static T Sum<T>(T[] xs) where T : INumber<T> { T acc = T.Zero; foreach (var x in xs) acc += x; return acc; } Console.WriteLine($"{Sum(new[]{1,2,3,4})} {Sum(new[]{1.5,2.5,3.0})}");

cards.prompt: A custom static abstract interface member IZero<T>.Zero with the self-constraint T : IZero<T>: Money implements it, so Id<Money>() resolves T.Zero to Money.Zero and Cents is 0, printing 0
cards.code: using System; interface IZero<T> where T : IZero<T> { static abstract T Zero { get; } } static T Id<T>() where T : IZero<T> => T.Zero; struct Money : IZero<Money> { public int Cents; public static Money Zero => new Money{Cents=0}; } Console.WriteLine($"{Id<Money>().Cents}");
